ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON FEB-06-2007 IN 206, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF WAS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 36(A)&(D). THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 5/29/07 @ 11:15AM IN DEPT. 206; SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE SET FOR 4/25/07 @ 1:30PM IN DEPT. 514. PLAINTIFF'S DEMAND IS DUE BY 4/11/07. DEFENDANTS' COUNTER DEMAND IS DUE BY 4/18/07. INTERROGATORIES/FRICTION CLAIM AND VERIFICATION ARE DUE IN 20 DAYS. DISCOVERY IS OPEN UNTIL THE CASE IS ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL COURT. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS STIPULATED TO. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE ALL PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AND CAUSATION WITNESSES AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 3/8/07, AND SHALL ALSO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS THEY INTEND TO USE AT TRIAL AS TO PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY THAT DATE. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. DEFENDANTS TO PROVIDE INFORMATION REGARDING PROPOSITION 51 WITNESSES AND AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES BY 3/28/07. IF DEFENDANTS LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. ALL ECONOMIC EXPERT DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, INCLUDING DEPOSITION OF ECONOMIC EXPERT. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE FAXED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. NO LATER THAN 12 DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, EACH SIDE MAY IDENTIFY UP TO THREE EXPERT WITNESSES PERCEIVED TO BE NECESSARY TO BE DEPOSED FOR MEANINGFUL SETTLEMENT DISCUSSIONS TO OCCUR. THESE EXPERTS WILL BE DEPOSED PRIOR TO THE TRIAL DATE. TRIAL WILL NOT BE DELAYED FOR THE PURPOSE OF DEPOSING EXPERTS. WRITTEN ORDER PENDING. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: D. LOK; REPORTER: JOYCE LONG CSR #8620 (206)

APPEARANCES:
Oren P. Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Rya D. Holliday, et al.
Kathryn J. LaFevers, Esq. of GORDON & REES (415-986-5900), appearing for defendant / opposing party Gibbs & Cox, Inc.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS RAY HOLLIDAY, SHIRLEY HOLLIDAY, MARY MCCASTON, RAY HOLLIDAY, JOSEPH HOLLIDAY, KAYE FREEMAN'S MOTION FOR ORDER SEVERING DEFENDANT GIBBS & COX, INC. FROM CASE
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for order severing defendant Gibbs & Cox from this case is denied. While the court is sympathetic to the rationale for the motion, severing a single defendant and staying the action as to that defendant pending the decision on appeal in another case raises a host of possible adverse case management issues given the uncertainty of when the court of appeal will issue a decision on the Baragar appeal and the possible running of the 5 year statute.
The Court further granted plaintiffs' request to extend time for filing the opposition to defendant Gibbs & Cox, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment. The Court continued the hearing from January 5, 2010 to January 13, 2010 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 220. Plaintiff's opposition due by 12/23 and defendant's reply due by 1/10/2011. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
